authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552 - Public information; agency rules, opinions, orders, records, and proceedings
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. Code source for FOIA, the federal public-records statute that governs access, exemptions, request processing, fees, appeal, and judicial-review posture for federal agency records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Request intake, response-clock tracking, access posture, disclosure-authority routing, and appeal-boundary handling
used_for: FOIA request, exemption, fee, timing, and appeal terminology
boundary between administrative records support and final disclosure or withholding authority
required scenario families for public-records access conflicts and legal interpretation escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 552a - Records maintained on individuals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. Code source for Privacy Act systems of records, individual access, amendment, disclosure accounting, and protected record handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy, system-of-record, individual-record, disclosure accounting, and amendment escalation
used_for: rights-sensitive amendment and access scenario design

authoritative_sources.title: 44 U.S.C. 3101 and 3301 - Federal agency records management and records definition
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. Code source for agency recordkeeping duties and the statutory definition of records that separates official records from nonrecord convenience copies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Official-record classification, custody validation, inventory, and nonrecord boundary review
used_for: failure modes around treating unsupported material as official or complete

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Records Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority and publishes official records-management guidance for schedules, disposition, transfer, retrieval, and records-program responsibilities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Records schedule, retention, disposition, transfer, access, and retrieval governance
used_for: governance escalation for missing schedules or disposition authority

authoritative_sources.title: 36 CFR Part 1220 - Federal Records; General
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Current federal records regulations define records-management responsibilities, terms, and baseline operating expectations for agency records programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Records-management role, recordkeeping responsibility, definition, and operating-control review

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Agency FOIA Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice, Office of Information Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ OIP is responsible for encouraging agency FOIA compliance and provides official guidance for agency FOIA regulations, including request intake, routing, requester contact, timing, fees, and identity handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Request intake, triage, requester communication, timing, fee, and routing support
used_for: boundary between administrative communications and official FOIA decisions

authoritative_sources.title: FAR 4.801 - Government Contract Files
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Current FAR source for contract-file documentation, defining the contract file as a complete transaction history supporting decisions, reviews, investigations, litigation, and congressional inquiries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Procurement-file record validation, transaction-history preservation, and procurement-authority routing
used_for: routing when procurement approval or procurement-sensitive disclosure is requested

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized federal internal-control standard for documentation, segregation of duties, control activities, monitoring, evidence quality, and auditability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audit-trail, control ownership, evidence sufficiency, monitoring, and segregation-of-duty review
used_for: segregation between administrative preparation and official approval authority
domain failure modes for missing evidence and unsupported official changes

authoritative_sources.title: Office of Government Information Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OGIS is the FOIA ombuds office created by Congress to review agency FOIA policies, procedures, and compliance and to resolve FOIA disputes between requesters and agencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: FOIA dispute, requester communication, compliance review, and escalation routing
used_for: refusal triggers when appeal, mediation, or dispute resolution authority is requested

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 555 - Ancillary matters
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Administrative Procedure Act text that anchors prompt notice, representation, and written denial requirements when records support agency matters, denials, appeals, or participant-facing decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Notice, denial, appeal, representation, and rights-sensitive escalation
used_for: boundary between record maintenance and adjudication or legal decision

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request scope, requester correspondence, jurisdiction, agency or program context, due date, requested action, custody facts, public-records rule, Privacy Act indicators, and authority owner before any records status is asserted.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Official-record identification and retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether responsive materials are official records, nonrecords, convenience copies, case-file records, procurement records, notices, correspondence, public-service decisions, or transparency logs; tie each item to a system of record and custodian.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Search, evidence, and correspondence package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Maintain search plans, search logs, custodian notes, correspondence, due dates, responsive-record inventories, document attachments, and audit trails needed for downstream review.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Validation and administrative maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Validate whether records are complete, current, properly classified, traceable to authoritative systems, and ready for administrative use while preserving missing evidence and conflicting source states.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, privacy, and publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Check retention schedules, disposition authority, access restrictions, Privacy Act flags, PII, protected records, procurement sensitivity, transparency-log requirements, publication prerequisites, and preservation holds before disclosure or movement.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Escalation, due-process, and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Route disclosure determinations, exemption analysis, redactions, Privacy Act amendments, appeal decisions, legal interpretation, adjudication, enforcement, procurement, audit opinions, retention disposition, and conflicting source-policy questions to the proper owner.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

artifacts: public-records request, FOIA request, state public-records request, requester correspondence, acknowledgement, clarification request, and due-date log
search plan, search cut, search log, custodian notes, records location map, responsive-record inventory, and no-records evidence

official records, nonrecord or convenience-copy flags, case files, applications, eligibility records, permits, notices, correspondence, public-service decisions, procurement documents, and transparency logs
system of records notice context, Privacy Act access or amendment request, disclosure accounting, PII markers, protected-record flags, and consent or identity-verification evidence
records schedule, retention period, disposition authority, litigation or preservation hold, access classification, redaction review marker, exemption-review marker, and publication checklist

administrative status note, document or records completeness summary, documentation completeness summary, record update note, evidence packet, governance escalation note, and audit-trail or exception summary
systems_of_record: records management or electronic document and records management system
identity, consent, privacy, or system-of-records register
audit, logging, legal hold, and compliance evidence repository

allowed: Classify public-records administration requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate whether required records, attachments, source context, retrieval hooks, custodians, system references, and owner facts are present for administrative use.
Prepare completeness summaries, status notes, evidence packets, search-log packages, record update notes, audit-trail summaries, and governance escalation packages.
Determine whether records appear complete, current, properly classified, and traceable to authoritative systems for the requested administrative use.

Route missing records, conflicting records, retention issues, privacy issues, access issues, disclosure decisions, adjudication decisions, legal review, procurement review, or audit review to the correct owner.
escalate: Required jurisdiction, agency policy, public-records rule, records schedule, custodian, official source, authority map, or tenant source hierarchy is missing.
FOIA, state public-records law, Privacy Act, NARA records guidance, FAR, agency policy, tenant policy, or system-of-record data materially conflict.

Disclosure, withholding, exemption, redaction, appeal, fee waiver, expedited processing, Privacy Act amendment, publication, deletion, retention disposition, legal interpretation, adjudication, enforcement, procurement, or audit opinion affects the requested outcome.
Eligibility, rights, benefits, public obligations, notice, appeal windows, or due-process posture depend on record content or legal interpretation.
Record retention, access, publication, legal hold, preservation, or protected-information handling cannot be resolved from documented authority.
refuse: Request asks the lane to disclose, withhold, redact, amend, destroy, delete, publish, close, or certify official records without documented authority.

Request asks for legal interpretation, final FOIA or public-records determination, Privacy Act amendment decision, appeal decision, adjudication, enforcement posture, audit opinion, procurement decision, financial approval, or policy override.
Required records, authoritative context, retrieval hooks, or audit trail are missing and would change the outcome.
Stakeholder asks to alter official records without source-of-truth confirmation, authority, and auditable change trail.
Request would bypass retention, privacy, access, due-process, procurement-sensitive, protected-record, litigation-hold, public-transparency, or audit controls.

domain_failure_modes: Treating administrative completeness as final FOIA disclosure, exemption, withholding, redaction, Privacy Act amendment, appeal, or legal determination.
Changing official record status, classification, transparency-log state, retention marker, or request closure without delegated authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and audit trail.
Proceeding when jurisdiction, agency policy, public-records rule, records schedule, records custodian, source hierarchy, or retrieval hook is missing or conflicting.
Suppressing conflicting system records instead of preserving both states and escalating to the official owner.
Publishing, deleting, destroying, or externally sharing records without resolving retention, privacy, access, procurement-sensitive, litigation-hold, or protected-information constraints.
Failing to preserve notice, correspondence, appeal-window, amendment-request, disclosure-accounting, or denial evidence where rights, benefits, public obligations, or due process are affected.
Issuing audit, procurement, enforcement, adjudication, or legal conclusions rather than assembling evidence and routing to the proper authority.

Accepting tenant terminology such as search cut, Vaughn index, SORN, convenience copy, official record, transparency log, or redaction review without mapping to artifacts, owners, and systems of record.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

required_workflow_questions.question: What statutory, eligibility, or due-process rules govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: FOIA, Privacy Act, Federal Records Act, APA, DOJ OIP guidance, NARA records guidance, FAR contract-file rules, OGIS references, and tenant jurisdictional public-records rules govern the lane. The lane collects and packages facts but escalates legal interpretation, official disclosure, amendment, retention disposition, appeal, adjudication, or enforcement decisions.

required_workflow_questions.question: What records must exist for the work to be auditable and fair?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The request file, scope and due-date log, correspondence, search plan, search log, custodian notes, responsive-record inventory, official source-system references, retention and access markers, transparency-log state, privacy flags, owner map, and audit logs must exist or be explicitly recorded as missing.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and adjudication, enforcement, or legal authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before final disclosure, withholding, redaction, exemption, Privacy Act amendment, appeal, retention disposition, legal interpretation, adjudication, enforcement, procurement decision, audit opinion, financial approval, or policy override authority.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific public-records law and state or local exemptions
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Tenant must provide jurisdiction, public-records statute, agency manual, source hierarchy, disclosure authority, and legal-review model before final use.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Final disclosure, exemption, redaction, Privacy Act amendment, appeal, and retention disposition authority
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Spec treats these as outside lane authority and routes to disclosure officer, privacy officer, records officer, legal, adjudication, or authorized human reviewer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Conflicting records across portals, repositories, email archives, and case systems
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Spec requires preserving conflicting states and escalating source-of-truth resolution instead of synthesizing a final official status.
